objective: Matched post-rewrite evaluation of the lexer-native compiler tree after removing literal-specific candidate filters.
conclusion: Removing literal-specific filters preserved the deterministic lexical path but did not produce complete semantic programs. Lark reachability admits partial expression continuations; the model then extends them until the canvas cap or a compiler dead end. The next lever is generated AST/schema completion state, with no token/component-specific exclusions. This is a negative diagnostic result, not a ship claim.
